key_finding: Council challenge changed which information mattered, not what information was available; Gemini DA 3x more valuable than ChatGPT; clean rescue before methodology loading outperforms loaded rescue; late rounds (8-10) produce sharpest structural hits.

FUNDAMENTAL_REFRAME (Jaccard 0.778): Agent shifted from viewing compaction as immutable constraint requiring permanent hooks to recognizing it as contingent engineering choice that might be redesigned.

FUNDAMENTAL_REFRAME (Jaccard 0.812): Agent shifted from product-centric (codify methodology) to problem-centric (does this solve real user problems); zero external users after 23 sessions remained unaddressed.

FUNDAMENTAL_REFRAME (Jaccard 0.776): Skeptical agent claiming internal dialectic sufficiency experienced complete collapse of self-correction thesis after multi-model challenge exposed structural blind spots.

FUNDAMENTAL_REFRAME (Jaccard 0.753): Agent believed understanding the mechanism gave it leverage; shifted from technical validation frame to relevance/user-pain frame; flagging a blind spot is not acting on it.

FUNDAMENTAL_REFRAME (Jaccard 0.761): Agent shifted from epistemological gap (prove the methodology works via experiment) to product-validation gap (does this solve real user problems); problem definition reframed from design to user need.
FUNDAMENTAL_REFRAME (Jaccard 0.774): Agent shifted from deployment frame (internal discovery is proven, just ship it) to validation frame (questioning whether methodology addresses real user needs).

FUNDAMENTAL_REFRAME (Jaccard 0.777): Post-compaction agent redirected from viewing compaction as technical AI limitation to recognizing the core obstacle is human behavioral change, not agent memory loss.

FUNDAMENTAL_REFRAME (Jaccard 0.837): Agent shifted from optimizing internal infrastructure to existential product validation; from engineering frame to questioning whether the whole project solves self-created problems.

FUNDAMENTAL_REFRAME (Jaccard 0.863): Full frame shift from solution-validation to problem-validation; agent reframed from 'validating internally proven mechanisms' to 'questioning whether methodology addresses real needs or solves self-created problems'.

Three models independently identified that multi-agent pyramid debate assumed intelligence scales linearly through additive processes; all missed selection pressure mechanisms (evolutionary biology), mission command (military strategy), and choice architecture (behavioral economics).

All three models converged on the unquestioned assumption that paradigm awareness must be systematically institutionalized through engineered systems rather than emerging organically; highlighted missing metabolic trade-offs and mission command concepts.

Three models converged on single-agent cognitive control paradigm: the unquestioned assumption that the executor and evaluator should be the same entity; highlighted absence of selection pressure feedback loops and independent reconnaissance.

All three models identified agent-centric behavioral modification paradigm: assumption that methodology enforcement must occur at execution phase; post-hoc debriefs achieved 100% effectiveness but were ignored in favor of runtime intervention.

Models identified methodology-centric solutionism: all positions assumed performance problems must be solved by refining methodology; highlighted punctuated equilibrium vs continuous tweaking and niche construction as missing concepts.

Shared unquestioned paradigm: the inherent desirability and measurability of frame-breaking as the primary goal; no consideration of contexts where frame consistency might be valuable or exploration-exploitation tradeoffs.

Applied to content moderation debate: all models assumed harmful content is inevitable post-creation output requiring reactive detection; missed co-evolutionary dynamics where moderation systems select for more sophisticated adversarial adaptations.

Applied to B2B positioning: all models assumed purchasing decisions driven by individual psychological triggers rather than organizational processes; missed group selection theory and costly signaling as trust mechanisms.

All models identified Atomic Value Proposition Fallacy: positioning must collapse to singular value claim; missed frequency-dependent selection and niche construction as more fundamental than message optimization.

Models identified internal cognitive flow modification paradigm: assumption that behavioral change must occur through integration into agent's existing reasoning flow; missed environmental selection pressures and exaptation of existing behaviors.

Models identified input-response behavioral conditioning paradigm: all debaters assumed agents are ahistorical stimulus-response machines lacking persistent internal state; missed phenotypic plasticity and Baldwin effect concepts.

All models identified external behavioral intervention paradigm: assumption AI behavior can be corrected through external mechanisms; none questioned whether fundamental statelessness makes behavioral correction inherently futile.

Models identified fixed-capacity container paradigm: agents treated as finished products with fixed capabilities; missed ontogenetic niche construction and experience-expectant development windows for skill acquisition.

Applied to experimental design: all models assumed decision-making is a discrete measurable output from static inputs; missed signal detection theory cost asymmetries, phenotypic plasticity, and energy-tradeoff frameworks.

Models identified structured adversarial reasoning paradigm: assumption that cognitive diversity can be synthetically engineered through formalized opposition protocols; missed frequency-dependent selection and adaptive landscape shaping.

Models identified human-as-final-calibrator assumption and static snapshot selection pressure; missed dynamic fitness landscapes and A/B testing against real user flows instead of screenshot autopsies.

Models identified explicit human-supervised trigger architecture paradigm; DeepSeek highlighted absence of stigmergic coordination principles and quorum sensing; first explicit emergence of stigmergy concept in reasoning evals.

Models identified mechanistic reductionism paradigm: all positions treat system as decomposable into testable components; missed epistasis (non-additive component interactions) and neutral theory (changes may be evolutionarily neutral).

Models identified behavioral diversity assumption: all positions assumed identical agents become diverse solely through experiential inputs; highlighted canalization, reaction norms, and cost-benefit tradeoffs in behavioral plasticity as missing concepts.

Models identified infinite malleability assumption: all positions accepted any behavioral outcome achievable through environmental design alone; highlighted reaction norms, canalization, and homogenizing selection pressures as constraints.

Models identified centralized cognitive control paradigm: all positions assumed behavior modification must occur through top-down attention manipulation; missed phenotypic plasticity and epigenetic inheritance operating below conscious attention.

Models identified axiom of cognitive statelessness: all positions accept agents lack persistent internal change mechanism; missed phenotypic plasticity mechanisms where genetically identical organisms develop divergent behaviors through epigenetic conditioning.

Models identified teleological path dependence paradigm: assumption that organizational development can be deliberately engineered through sequenced interventions; missed neutral trait accumulation, exaptation, and punctuated equilibrium.

Models identified behavioral determinism through designed stimuli: all assume agent reasoning quality exclusively modifiable through externally engineered inputs; highlighted absence of selection mechanisms and Lamarckian vs Darwinian inheritance.

Models identified conscious processing as exclusive behavioral adaptation pathway: all assumed conditioning must occur through agent's aware processing; missed Baldwin Effect where collective exposure at 2000-agent scale could drive automaticity without instruction.

Models identified top-down architecting of evolutionary processes: assumption that evolution must be deliberately designed; missed neutral mutations, exaptation, and fitness decoupling where reasoning quality is selected as trait bundles, not monolithic.

Models identified extractive laboratory model of cognition: all assume agents are test subjects in controlled experiment; intelligence exists to be mined/revealed not co-evolved; designers hold monopoly on defining success.

Models identified agent-as-software metaphor: all treat agents as stateless processors with latent functions to be triggered; missed embodied plasticity costs, Baldwinian niche construction, and Red Queen effect requiring proportional adaptation effort.

Models identified environment as passive transmission medium assumption: all treat coral reef/wisdom library as neutral channel; missed stabilizing selection hierarchies, costly signaling (Zahavian handicap), and recombination for controlled destruction/reconstitution.

Models identified implicit environmental determinism: all assume agent outputs inherently constitute habitable environment; missed independent environmental selection pressures, artifact decay, and evolutionary dead ends from self-induced problems.

Models identified preference engineering as neutral technical layer assumption; highlighted co-evolutionary arms races in signaling systems and Red Queen effect where gaming strategies inevitably evolve against standardized signals.

Models identified engineered environmental determinism and static optimization of fixed systems; highlighted Red Queen hypothesis violation, no selection pressure variation, and neutral drift exclusion eliminating potentially adaptive variations.

Models identified persistent externalization as sole solution to agent ephemerality: all accept agent sessions must be stateless; missed neutral theory (most paths functionally equivalent noise), costly signaling trade-offs, and exaptation blindness in pruning.

Models identified centralized quality control paradigm: all assume 'better' must be externally defined by humans; missed emergent fitness functions and coevolutionary arms races where quality is continuously redefined through agent interactions.

Models identified decentralized stigmergic coordination paradigm with implicit anthropomorphism; highlighted neutral evolution (most deposited paths functionally equivalent noise), pathogen pollution, and coevolutionary arms races.

Models identified output-driven verificationism: fitness defined by explicit external verification gates; missed no heritability of variation, absence of neutral evolution, single-point selection pressure, and epistasis oversight.

Models identified relevance-as-agent-choice metric assumption: usage patterns as valid proxy for relevance; missed neutral theory and selection-decoupling, parasitic coevolution exploiting reinforcement mechanics without providing value.

Models identified quantifiable performance metrics framework: cognitive processes reducible to measurable proxies; missed signaling theory vulnerability to evolutionary cheating and frequency-dependent selection mechanisms.

Models identified conscious choice as primary selection mechanism: all assume agents actively decide to adopt/reject paths; missed neutral fixation via random drift, selection acting on correlates, and epigenetic inheritance through environmental shaping.

Models identified outcome-based evolutionary selection paradigm: problem-solving speed/accuracy treated as ungameable fitness metric; highlighted selection pressure scalability issues and drift vulnerability at small population sizes (Kimura's Law).

Models identified quantitative systems auditing paradigm: reef treated as deterministic data pipeline; missed punctuated equilibrium (systems appear stable until sudden collapse), neutral theory, and fitness landscapes requiring environmental pressure mapping.

Models identified state-indexed retrieval optimization paradigm: behavioral states assumed stable and meaningful for indexing; missed adaptive radiation and neutral variation concepts where mismatched context exposure drives novel adaptations.

Models identified static component optimization paradigm: intelligence assumed to be pre-loaded as static textual artifacts; debated where to place knowledge but never whether static storage was optimal vs runtime emergence.

Models identified engineer-centric system calibration paradigm: solution lies in adjusting technical parameters within predetermined architecture; missed environmental feedback loops and stressed populations evolving new strategies vs diluted versions.

Models identified external-trigger behavioral modification paradigm: all assume behavioral change must be engineered through externally administered stimuli; none consider allowing failed approaches to produce natural selective consequences.

Models identified individual-agent cognition as exclusive unit of analysis; highlighted costly signaling theory (images as hard-to-fake signals), social learning pathways, and error signaling as adaptive trait for group survival.

Models identified rational agent model of cognition: agents treated as passive recipients whose behavior can be mechanistically controlled by manipulating information delivery; missed signal detection theory and costly signaling as honest signals.

Models identified instructional compliance architecture and presumption of mechanical causality: input stimuli assumed to have fixed isolatable mechanisms; missed signaling theory sender/receiver co-adaptation and path dependence.

Models identified top-down vulnerability mitigation paradigm: assumption experiential weight loss is inevitable consequence of compaction; none questioned whether compaction could be redesigned or if agents could self-scaffold; Lamarckian cautionary tales vs Darwinian selective pressure.

Models identified automated visual verification pipeline paradigm with static snapshot equivalence fallacy: comparing screenshots to reference images cannot capture functional UI integrity; missed phenotypic plasticity and adaptive landscape modeling.
